channel.title: NYT > World > Americas
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Escalates Canada Tariffs as Mark Carney Holds Firm
item.description: The president’s new tariffs on a top U.S. trading partner aim at gaining concessions, but the Canadian government, and the public, have been bracing for the escalation.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:description: Steel coils at the Algoma Steel facility in Ontario in July. U.S. tariffs on goods such as steel, aluminum and vehicles have hit sensitive Canadian sectors.

item.title: The Teenage Boys In Search of Their Mother After the Venezuela Earthquake
item.description: After Venezuela’s devastating earthquakes, Winder and Deivi Delfín dug every day for 26 days to find the bodies of their mother, brother and grandmother.
item.dc:creator: Julie Turkewitz and Adriana Loureiro Fernandez
item.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Winder Delfín, 17, right, and his brother Deivi, 15, during the 24th day of searching for their mother, younger brother and grandmother, who lived on the first floor of a twelve story building that collapsed during the twin earthquakes last month.

item.title: At Least 27 Dead in Guyana After Ferry Capsizes
item.description: Dozens of others are feared dead as crews scour for bodies after the vessel sank on its way to a gold-mining outpost in the South American country.
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero
item.media:description: A handout image showing rescue efforts on Sunday after a ferry carrying scores of people capsized off the coast of Guyana in South America.

item.title: Daniel Ortega, President of Nicaragua, Says He Plans to Abolish Elections
item.description: Daniel Ortega has been accused of rigging recent elections in Nicaragua. Now, the authoritarian leader says the nation won’t even hold a vote.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas and James Wagner
item.media:description: President Daniel Ortega, 80, who with his wife has exerted complete control over Nicaragua for many years, at a conference in 2014.

item.title: Trump to Impose 50% Tariff on Many Canadian Goods
item.description: The administration will use an untested legal provision to put significant duties on Canadian exports, reigniting a clash with one of America’s biggest trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Ian Austen
item.media:description: The tariffs, which go into effect in 30 days, would be imposed on a range of Canadian exports, including wine and dairy products.

item.title: Evacuees of Ontario’s Wildfires Wait and Worry
item.description: Flames crept, and then sprinted, toward unsuspecting residents who fled remote parts of northwestern Ontario. Now they want to know why officials weren’t prepared.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai and Cole Burston
item.media:credit: Cole Burston for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lyndon Paavola showing footage on his phone from his evacuation alongside community members from Namaygoosisagagun First Nation due to fast spreading wildfire that destroyed his community.

item.title: In Venezuela’s Rubble, a Search for the Family Pets
item.description: Volunteers are reuniting pets with families, offering Venezuelans comfort amid loss and devastation.
item.dc:creator: Tibisay Romero and Genevieve Glatsky
item.media:description: A firefighter rescuing a dog from a building in Caracas that collapsed after twin earthquakes last month.

item.title: Tears in Argentina for Lionel Messi’s ‘Last Tango’
item.description: There were still fireworks and smoke bombs, but the nation was disappointed for its soccer star after he lost in his likely last World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola
item.media:credit: Sebastián López Brach for The New York Times
item.media:description: A watch party in Rosario, Argentina, Lionel Messi’s hometown, turned somber.

item.title: Earthquake in Peru Kills 6 and Displaces Hundreds
item.description: Peru is prone to frequent quakes, but the 5.5-magnitude temblor was the biggest to strike the central highlands area in more than 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Mitra Taj
item.media:description: Una casa en Chupuro, Perú, que fue destruida por el terremoto el sábado.

item.title: When Will the Wildfire Smoke Clear?
item.description: Forecasters expected the worst conditions across the Great Lakes region on Sunday, with air quality alerts in effect across the area.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff and Nazaneen Ghaffar
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke from wildfires in Ontario and Minnesota, pictured in downtown Chicago on Thursday.

item.title: Argentines, Already ‘Losing Their Minds,’ Brace for World Cup Final
item.description: A championship victory would be pure elation, but Argentina is already basking in a state of grace after a win against England and a monthlong fever dream.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola, Natalie Alcoba, Lucía Cholakian Herrera and Daniel Politi
item.media:credit: Sebastián López Brach for The New York Times
item.media:description: Thousands of people celebrated Argentina’s victory over England, and its qualification for the World Cup final, on Wednesday in Rosario, Argentina.

item.title: MetLife Stadium Faces Monumental Last Test in Sunday’s World Cup Final
item.description: New Jersey’s N.F.L. stadium is used to big events. So far, it’s seen few issues during the World Cup, but Sunday’s final presents the biggest challenge yet.
item.dc:creator: David Waldstein
item.media:description: Fans at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, N.J., before the start of the World Cup final between Argentina and Spain on Sunday.

item.title: Canada Wildfires: What to Know
item.description: Hundreds of wildfires in Canada and the United States have blanketed large sections of North America in dense smoke over the past week.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Ian Willms for The New York Times
item.media:description: The observation deck of Toronto’s CN Tower on Wednesday, as wildfire smoke enveloped downtown.

item.title: Wildfires Continue To Burn in Ontario, Canada, Sending Smoke to U.S. Cities
item.description: Nearly 200 fires continued to devastate swaths of the province as smoke continued to drift across parts of Canada and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:credit: Cole Burston for The New York Times
item.media:description: Premier Doug Ford of Ontario after a news conference in Thunder Bay on Saturday. Roughly 1.7 million acres have burned in Ontario, data from the Canadian Interagency Forest Fire Center shows.

item.title: Most Forest Fires in Canada Are Simply Impossible to Put Out
item.description: Half of Canada’s wildfires are in remote areas with no road access. Impossible to fight, they are often left to burn.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi
item.media:description: A wildfire burning in Bison Lake, Alberta, last year.

item.title: Freedom Convoy Leader, Under House Arrest, Wants to Visit the White House
item.description: Prosecutors say Tamara Lich wants a “blank cheque” for international travel, including a cruise, arguing that she is trying to profit from the very protest that got her sentenced.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:description: The Freedom Convoy organizer Tamara Lich at her sentencing hearing last summer. She is asking a court to let her travel to the United States.

item.title: Iqaluit, Canada’s Most Northern City, Gets Its First Bus Line
item.description: Jacinto Marques left Angola for Canada’s frozen edge. Now he’s running a bus service in Iqaluit, the largest, and only city in Canada’s Arctic.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi and Renaud Philippe
item.media:description: Looking out the window of an Iqaluit Transit bus during its route through the city.

item.title: Trump Threatens Canada With Tariffs for Wildfire Smoke Over U.S.
item.description: The president said on Friday in a social media post that Canada was failing to manage its wildfire response as smoke has blanketed U.S. cities including New York and Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen and Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:description: President Trump departing Joint Base Andrews, en route to New York, on Friday.

item.title: How Athletes at the World Cup and Beyond Celebrate Victories: Dances, Doughnuts and Finger Wags
item.description: The World Cup has brought renewed attention to the art of celebration. Here’s a look at all kinds of athletes whose rituals of triumph became part of their legacies.
item.dc:creator: Amisha Padnani
item.media:description: Rashidi Yekini celebrating after he scored Nigeria’s first-ever World Cup goal in 1994.

item.title: Prominent Cuban Artist Will Go Into Exile After 5-Year Prison Term
item.description: The United States offered a visa to Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, who was expected to fly to Miami on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: David C. Adams
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara in Havana in 2021.

item.title: When Will the Smoke End? Our Meteorologist Explains Why It’s Complicated.
item.description: The interplay between the burning fires, the smoke itself and quickly changing weather patterns is making the next few days more uncertain than usual.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Wildfire smoke hung over pickleball players in Manhattan on Thursday. While the smoke decreased in the city early Friday, it could return later as weather patterns change.

item.title: 7.3-Magnitude Earthquake Off Coast of Mexico and Guatemala Poses Tsunami Threat
item.description: A powerful earthquake near the border of Mexico and Guatemala raised fears of a tsunami and shook buildings across three countries.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.media:description: People outside the Biblioteca Nacional in San Salvador, El Salvador, following a 7.4 magnitude earthquake on Friday.

item.title: Cuban Dissident Was Missing A Week After Prison Release
item.description: A Cuban artist awaiting permission from the Trump administration to enter the United States was held in an unknown location in Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara in 2018.

item.title: The Burning, Bleeding, Breathing Art of Ana Mendieta
item.description: A Tate Modern survey of the artist’s lifetime of work sidesteps chronology to focus on her engagement with nature. But does that give us the whole picture?
item.dc:creator: Emily LaBarge

item.title: Venezuela Earthquakes Force a Grim Homecoming for Migrants
item.description: Mass migration, which defines modern Venezuela, has heavily marked the earthquake, too, saving many who had left, but also making their grief harder.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola and Adriana Loureiro Fernandez
item.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Liliana Figueroa, who had migrated to Brazil, returned to Venezuela after her daughter, Angelina, was killed in the earthquake.

item.title: Canada’s Wildfires Are Burning Through the Night
item.description: As overnight temperatures climb across Canada, fires that once spread more slowly after dark are burning around the clock, straining fire crews.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai and Amy Graff
item.media:credit: Ian Willms for The New York Times
item.media:description: The observation deck of the CN Tower on Wednesday evening as wildfire smoke enveloped downtown Toronto.

item.title: Canada Wildfires: See Photos of Smoke Shrouding NYC, Toronto and More Cities
item.description: The air was hazardous, and skylines at times seemed nearly erased. The smoke will linger before finally letting up.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Manhattan skyline on a hazy Thursday on the Brooklyn Bridge.

item.title: Wildfires Continue in Ontario as Officials Brace for More Evacuations
item.description: Ontario’s minister for emergency preparedness has asked the federal government for help, including potentially deploying Canadian soldiers to the affected regions.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni and Vjosa Isai
item.media:description: Smoke from wildfires in Ontario, Canada, turned Toronto’s skies orange on Wednesday.

item.title: As Argentina Holds Up Falklands Banner on Defeating England, Here’s What to Know About the War
item.description: Argentina and Britain fought a bloody war over the Falkland Islands in 1982. Decades later, the dispute suffuses matches between England and Argentina.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon

item.title: Maps Show Where Wildfire Smoke Will Darken Skies on Thursday
item.description: Air quality in several cities reached hazardous levels on Thursday morning as smoke was expected to move across North America.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones
item.media:description: Smoke gave the sky in Toronto an orange hue on Wednesday.

item.title: The Long-Running, Never-Ending Argument Over What Counts as ‘America’
item.description: The label applies to the country, but also two whole continents. Our claim over it is yet another way for us to annoy the entire hemisphere.
item.dc:creator: Nitsuh Abebe

item.title: Wildfires Rage in Ontario as Smoke Affects Toronto’s Air Quality
item.description: Air quality in Canada’s largest city was at times the worst in the world on Wednesday, as wildfires across Ontario sent smoke drifting into New York City and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:credit: Laura Proctor/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The CN Tower in Toronto on Wednesday as smoke from wildfires enveloped the city.

item.title: Wildfire Smoke Spreads Across the Northeast as Temperatures Spike
item.description: A heat dome is responsible for miserably high temperatures, and it’s also helping to push smoke from wildfires to the east.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones and Mark Walker

item.title: He Has a $25 Million Bounty on His Head but Is Also a U.S. Partner in Venezuela
item.description: A list of alleged crimes has not prevented Diosdado Cabello, and other Venezuelan officials, from working with a U.S. government that once hounded them.
item.dc:creator: Anatoly Kurmanaev
item.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Diosdado Cabello, Venezuela’s interior minister, welcomed a flight last year bringing Venezuelan migrants from Mexico.

item.title: On ‘Caribenya,’ Lido Pimienta Found a Lifeline
item.description: On Lido Pimienta’s new album, “Caribenya,” the Colombian Canadian musician sought a warmer, more complex depiction of Caribbean culture. Creating it helped her rediscover the beauty of living.
item.dc:creator: Carolina Abbott Galvão
item.media:credit: Tommy Keith for The New York Times
item.media:description: On Pimienta’s new album, images of dragonflies, rivers and the sea abound, as do depictions of fraught emotional relationships and critiques of colonialism.
